items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq edge higher as cool inflation data boosts Fed rate cut hopes
items.description: Investors were digesting a softer-than-expected inflation reading and assessing a US-China plan to salvage their trade truce.

items.title: The Rare Earth Rerun
items.description: Stocks rose Tuesday as traders squinted at Londonâs trade talks, decided the tariff drama was heading for a whole lot of nothing, and snapped up the opening-salvo losers.

items.title: S&P 500, foreign markets soar, bitcoin rallying: Market Takeaways
items.description: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) capped off Tuesday's trading session in positive territory with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite's record highs back in investors' sights. Yahoo Finance markets and data editor Jared Blikre comes on Asking for a Trend to outline the biggest market themes he is observing, including the outperformance of foreign markets as compared to US equities and the price action behind bitcoin (BTC-USD), ethereum (ETH-USD), and metals futures. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Asking for a Trend here.

items.title: It’s Harvest Time for Traders
items.description: Editor’s Note: Despite another chaotic month, the S&P 500 recorded its best May since 1990 – rising more than 6%. However, that’s nothing compared to the gains my colleague Jeff Clark made. Jeff’s short-term trades delivered a cumulative return of 129% in just five days in May (he’ll explain how in today’s guest article) – more than 20X higher than the S&P 500’s gain. And those returns are no fluke. Jeff is one of the country’s top “chaos” traders, so he thrives during volatile markets. He has s

items.title: NFIB Small Business Survey: Optimism Recovered Slightly In May
items.description: The NFIB Small Business Optimism Index increased for the first time in five months, rising to 98.8 in May.
